title: Measuring Domain Saturation for Fine‑Tuning Large Language Models
relevance: Introduces the Domain Saturation Index (DSI) methodology and provides baseline results across legal, medical, and financial datasets, directly supporting the case studies discussed.

heading: Legal Corpus: High Saturation, Diminishing Returns
body: When applying the DSI to a 50 GB corpus of U.S. case law, the index quickly plateaued at 0.78 after 10 M tokens, indicating that additional fine‑tuning data yielded marginal performance gains on downstream tasks such as contract clause extraction.

title: Interactive Dashboard: Domain Saturation Across Industries
description: A live visual tool that lets readers compare DSI curves for legal, medical, and financial corpora, toggle token thresholds, and see how saturation correlates with benchmark scores on domain‑specific tasks.

title: Domain Adaptation for Legal NLP – Stanford CS224N Lecture
caption: Professor Christopher Manning explains how saturation metrics guide data selection for legal language models.

heading: Medical Corpus: Balancing Saturation and Specialty Terminology
body: In a 30 GB corpus of radiology reports, the DSI peaked at 0.64, but performance on clinical entity recognition continued to improve up to 25 M tokens, suggesting that specialized terminology can still benefit from extra data even after general saturation is reached.
